use: Said morning and evening, for protection
title: Protection Through the Day
arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ ٱسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي ٱلسَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ
translit: Bismillāhil-ladhī lā yaḍurru maʿasmihi shayʾun fil-arḍi wa lā fis-samāʾi wa huwa as-Samīʿul-ʿAlīm

translations.en: In the name of Allah, with whose name nothing on earth or in heaven can cause harm. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
translations.ur: اللہ کے نام سے، جس کے نام کے ساتھ زمین یا آسمان میں کوئی چیز نقصان نہیں پہنچا سکتی۔ وہ سننے والا، جاننے والا ہے۔

whenToRecite: In the morning and again in the evening.
virtue: The Prophet ﷺ said whoever says it will not be afflicted by any harm.
benefit: Recite three times in the morning and three times in the evening. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever says this will not be afflicted by any harm.

use: The first words upon waking
title: Gratitude Upon Waking
arabic: ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ ٱلنُّشُورُ
translit: Alḥamdulillāhil-ladhī aḥyānā baʿda mā amātanā wa ilayhin-nushūr

translations.en: All praise is for Allah who gave us life after taking it from us, and to Him is the resurrection.
translations.ur: تمام تعریفیں اللہ کے لیے ہیں جس نے ہمیں موت دینے کے بعد زندگی عطا کی، اور اسی کی طرف لوٹنا ہے۔

context: Sleep is a kind of minor death, and waking a daily resurrection. This dua marks that gift.
whenToRecite: The first words upon waking.
benefit: Said immediately upon waking. Sleep is a minor death; waking is a daily resurrection — this dua marks that gift.

use: As the day closes
title: Evening Remembrance
arabic: أَمْسَيْنَا وَأَمْسَى ٱلْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ، وَٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ
translit: Amsaynā wa amsal-mulku lillāh, wal-ḥamdulillāh, lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah

translations.en: We have entered evening and with it all dominion belongs to Allah. All praise is for Allah. There is no deity except Allah, alone, with no partner.
translations.ur: ہم نے شام کی اور اللہ کی بادشاہی نے شام کی۔ تمام تعریف اللہ کے لیے ہے۔ اللہ کے سوا کوئی معبود نہیں، وہ اکیلا ہے، اس کا کوئی شریک نہیں۔

whenToRecite: After the Asr prayer, as the day closes.
context: The morning version of this remembrance begins with 'Aṣbaḥnā' in place of 'Amsaynā'.
benefit: Recite after Asr prayer. The morning version begins with 'Aṣbaḥnā' instead of 'Amsaynā'.

use: When you feel trapped in the dark
title: The Dua of Yunus ؑ — From Within Darkness
arabic: لَّا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنتَ سُبْحَٰنَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
translit: Lā ilāha illā anta subḥānaka innī kuntu minaẓ-ẓālimīn

translations.en: There is no deity except You; glorified are You. Indeed, I have been among the wrongdoers.
translations.ur: تیرے سوا کوئی معبود نہیں، تو پاک ہے۔ بے شک میں ظالموں میں سے تھا۔

context: A complete turning to Allah in a single breath: it declares His oneness, glorifies Him as far above any fault, and owns one's own wrongdoing. It is the prayer for any moment you feel trapped, with no way out but Him.
story: These are the words Prophet Yunus ؑ called out from within the belly of the great fish. The Quran recounts that he cried out from within the darknesses (understood by the classical commentators as the darkness of the night, the sea, and the belly of the fish), and that Allah answered: "So We responded to him and saved him from the distress. And thus do We save the believers." (Quran 21:87-88)
whenToRecite: In any moment of hardship, fear, or feeling trapped, when you need a way out and there seems to be none.
virtue: The Prophet ﷺ said that no Muslim ever calls upon Allah with this supplication of Yunus ؑ for anything except that Allah answers him. (Tirmidhi 3505)
benefit: The dua of Prophet Yunus from inside the whale. The Prophet ﷺ said no Muslim calls upon Allah with these words — without adding or removing — except that Allah responds.

use: When you feel outnumbered
title: When Overwhelmed
arabic: حَسْبُنَا ٱللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ ٱلْوَكِيلُ
translit: Ḥasbunallāhu wa niʿmal-wakīl

translations.en: Allah is sufficient for us, and He is the best Disposer of affairs.
translations.ur: اللہ ہمارے لیے کافی ہے، اور وہ بہترین کارساز ہے۔

story: The words of Ibrahim ؑ when he was thrown into the fire, and of the Prophet ﷺ when he was warned of a large gathering enemy.
whenToRecite: In any moment of fear or feeling outnumbered.
benefit: The words of Ibrahim ؑ when thrown into the fire, and of the Prophet ﷺ when warned of a large enemy. Recite in any moment of fear or feeling outnumbered.

use: When your resolve is slipping
title: For Steadfastness in Difficulty
arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا
translit: Rabbanā afrigh ʿalaynā ṣabran wa thabbit aqdāmanā

translations.en: Our Lord, pour upon us patience and plant our feet firmly.
translations.ur: اے ہمارے رب! ہم پر صبر انڈیل دے اور ہمارے قدموں کو ثابت رکھ۔

context: The word 'afrigh' (pour) suggests an overflowing, generous grant of patience rather than a measured one.
whenToRecite: When your resolve is weakening under pressure.
benefit: The word 'pour' (afrigh) suggests an overflowing, generous grant of patience. Recite when your resolve is weakening under pressure.

use: At the bedside of someone ill
title: For Healing from Illness
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ النَّاسِ، أَذْهِبِ الْبَأْسَ، اشْفِ أَنْتَ الشَّافِي، لَا شِفَاءَ إِلَّا شِفَاؤُكَ، شِفَاءً لَا يُغَادِرُ سَقَمًا
translit: Allāhumma Rabban-nāsi, adh-hibil-baʾsa, ishfi anta ash-Shāfī, lā shifāʾa illā shifāʾuka, shifāʾan lā yughādiru saqamā

translations.en: O Allah, Lord of mankind, remove the affliction and grant healing — for You are the Healer. There is no cure except Your cure: a healing that leaves no illness behind.
translations.ur: اے اللہ! انسانوں کے رب، تکلیف کو دور کر دے، شفا دے — تو ہی شفا دینے والا ہے۔ تیری شفا کے سوا کوئی شفا نہیں، ایسی شفا جو کوئی بیماری نہ چھوڑے۔

story: The Prophet ﷺ recited this while placing his right hand on the sick.
whenToRecite: For yourself, or while placing your hand on someone who is ill.
benefit: The Prophet ﷺ recited this while placing his right hand on the sick. Recite for yourself or place your hand on someone who is ill.

use: Placed where it hurts
title: For Relief from Pain
arabic: أَعُوذُ بِعِزَّةِ اللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ
translit: Aʿūdhu biʿizzatillāhi wa qudratihī min sharri mā ajidu wa uḥādhir

translations.en: I seek refuge in the might of Allah and His power from the evil of what I feel and what I fear.
translations.ur: میں اللہ کی عزت اور قدرت کی پناہ مانگتا ہوں اس برائی سے جو مجھے محسوس ہو رہی ہے اور جس سے میں ڈرتا ہوں۔

whenToRecite: Place your hand on the place of pain and recite.
story: The Prophet ﷺ taught this to a companion who then reported that his pain left him.
benefit: Place your hand on the area of pain and recite this seven times. The Prophet ﷺ taught this to a companion who reported his pain disappeared.

use: When grief sits heavy
title: For Mental Distress & Grief
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي عَبْدُكَ، ابْنُ عَبْدِكَ، ابْنُ أَمَتِكَ، نَاصِيَتِي بِيَدِكَ، أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تَجْعَلَ الْقُرْآنَ رَبِيعَ قَلْبِي، وَنُورَ صَدْرِي، وَجَلَاءَ حُزْنِي، وَذَهَابَ هَمِّي
translit: Allāhumma innī ʿabduka, ibnu ʿabdika, ibnu amatika, nāṣiyatī biyadika ... an tajʿalal-Qurʾāna rabīʿa qalbī, wa nūra ṣadrī, wa jalāʾa ḥuznī, wa dhahāba hammī

translations.en: O Allah, I am Your servant, son of Your servant, son of Your maidservant. My forelock is in Your hand. I ask You to make the Quran the spring of my heart, the light of my chest, the lifting of my grief, and the departure of my anxiety.
translations.ur: اے اللہ! میں تیرا بندہ ہوں، تیرے بندے کا بیٹا، تیری بندی کا بیٹا۔ میری پیشانی تیرے ہاتھ میں ہے۔ میں تجھ سے سوال کرتا ہوں کہ قرآن کو میرے دل کی بہار، میرے سینے کا نور، میرے غم کا زوال، اور میری پریشانی کا خاتمہ بنا دے۔

context: One of the most comprehensive duas for grief and distress.
virtue: The Prophet ﷺ said that whoever says this, Allah will replace his sorrow with joy.
benefit: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this, Allah will replace his sorrow with joy.' One of the most comprehensive duas for depression and grief.

use: When you are searching for work
title: For Lawful Provision
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنِي بِحَلَالِكَ عَنْ حَرَامِكَ، وَأَغْنِنِي بِفَضْلِكَ عَمَّنْ سِوَاكَ
translit: Allāhumma-kfinī biḥalālika ʿan ḥarāmika, wa aghninī bifaḍlika ʿamman siwāk

translations.en: O Allah, suffice me through what is lawful so I have no need of what is unlawful, and enrich me through Your grace so I need no one but You.
translations.ur: اے اللہ! مجھے اپنی حلال روزی سے حرام سے بے نیاز کر دے، اور اپنے فضل سے مجھے اپنے سوا سب سے بے نیاز کر دے۔

context: It prioritizes lawfulness over abundance: asking to be made independent through what is permitted.
whenToRecite: When seeking employment or facing financial hardship.
benefit: One of the most recommended duas for those seeking employment or facing financial hardship. It prioritizes lawfulness over abundance.

use: Before something difficult
title: For Ease in a Difficult Task
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَا سَهْلَ إِلَّا مَا جَعَلْتَهُ سَهْلًا، وَأَنْتَ تَجْعَلُ الْحَزْنَ إِذَا شِئْتَ سَهْلًا
translit: Allāhumma lā sahla illā mā jaʿaltahu sahlā, wa anta tajʿalul-ḥazna idhā shiʾta sahlā

translations.en: O Allah, there is no ease except in that which You have made easy, and You make difficulty easy when You will.
translations.ur: اے اللہ! آسانی صرف وہی ہے جو تو نے آسان بنائی ہو، اور تو مشکل کو بھی آسان کر سکتا ہے جب چاہے۔

context: A reminder that ease comes from Allah, not from effort alone.
whenToRecite: Before a difficult task: an interview, a deadline, a hard conversation.
benefit: Recite before a difficult task — an interview, a deadline, a hard conversation. A reminder that ease comes from Allah, not effort alone.

use: When worry and debt weigh on you
title: Relief from Debt & Anxiety
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ، وَالْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَالْبُخْلِ وَالْجُبْنِ، وَضَلَعِ الدَّيْنِ، وَغَلَبَةِ الرِّجَالِ
translit: Allāhumma innī aʿūdhu bika minal-hammi wal-ḥazani, wal-ʿajzi wal-kasali, wal-bukhli wal-jubni, wa ḍalaʿid-dayni, wa ghalabatir-rijāl

translations.en: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from anxiety and grief, from weakness and laziness, from miserliness and cowardice, from the burden of debt and from being overpowered by men.
translations.ur: اے اللہ! میں تیری پناہ مانگتا ہوں غم اور اداسی سے، کمزوری اور سستی سے، بخل اور بزدلی سے، قرض کے بوجھ سے، اور لوگوں کے غلبے سے۔

whenToRecite: Said in the morning.
virtue: The Prophet ﷺ promised relief from worry and debt for those who recite it consistently.
context: Particularly fitting during job loss or a career transition.
benefit: Said in the morning. The Prophet ﷺ promised relief from worry and debt for those who recite this consistently. Particularly powerful during job loss or career transitions.

use: When you face a real decision
title: Istikhara — Seeking Allah's Choice
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْتَخِيرُكَ بِعِلْمِكَ، وَأَسْتَقْدِرُكَ بِقُدْرَتِكَ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ الْعَظِيمِ، فَإِنَّكَ تَقْدِرُ وَلَا أَقْدِرُ، وَتَعْلَمُ وَلَا أَعْلَمُ، وَأَنْتَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ
translit: Allāhumma innī astakhīruka biʿilmika, wa astaqdiruka biqudratika, wa asʾaluka min faḍlikal-ʿaẓīm, fa-innaka taqdiru wa lā aqdiru, wa taʿlamu wa lā aʿlamu, wa anta ʿallāmul-ghuyūb

translations.en: O Allah, I seek Your guidance through Your knowledge, and Your ability through Your power, and I ask of Your great bounty. For You are able while I am not, and You know while I do not, and You are the Knower of the unseen.
translations.ur: اے اللہ! میں تیرے علم کے ذریعے بہتری مانگتا ہوں، تیری قدرت سے طاقت مانگتا ہوں، اور تیرے عظیم فضل کا سوال کرتا ہوں۔ تو قادر ہے اور میں نہیں، تو جانتا ہے اور میں نہیں، اور تو غیب کا جاننے والا ہے۔

whenToRecite: Pray two voluntary rakʿahs, then recite.
context: Istikhara is not a dream oracle. It is asking Allah to incline your heart and your circumstances toward what is truly good.
benefit: Perform two voluntary rakʿahs, then recite. Istikhara is not a dream oracle — it is asking Allah to incline your heart and circumstances toward what is truly good.

use: Before you have to speak
title: For Clarity of Speech
arabic: رَبِّ ٱشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي وَٱحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِّن لِّسَانِي يَفْقَهُوا۟ قَوْلِي
translit: Rabbish-raḥ lī ṣadrī wa yassir lī amrī waḥlul ʿuqdatan min lisānī yafqahū qawlī

translations.en: My Lord, expand my chest, ease my task, and untie the knot of my tongue so they may understand my speech.
translations.ur: اے میرے رب! میرا سینہ کھول دے، میرے کام کو آسان کر، اور میری زبان کی گرہ کھول دے تاکہ لوگ میری بات سمجھیں۔

story: The dua of Musa ؑ before he faced Pharaoh.
whenToRecite: Before interviews, presentations, or hard conversations, anywhere clarity of speech matters.
benefit: The dua of Musa ؑ before facing Pharaoh. Recite before interviews, presentations, hard conversations — anywhere clarity of speech matters.

use: For the people you love
title: For a Righteous Spouse & Offspring
arabic: رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَٰجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّٰتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍ وَٱجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
translit: Rabbanā hab lanā min azwājinā wa dhurriyyātinā qurrata aʿyunin wajʿalnā lil-muttaqīna imāmā

translations.en: Our Lord, grant us from among our spouses and offspring comfort to our eyes, and make us a leader for the righteous.
translations.ur: اے ہمارے رب! ہمیں ہماری بیویوں اور اولاد سے آنکھوں کی ٹھنڈک عطا فرما، اور ہمیں متقیوں کا امام بنا دے۔

story: The dua of the servants of the Most Merciful, in Surah al-Furqan.
context: 'Qurrata aʿyun', comfort to the eyes, is among the highest expressions of joy in Arabic.
benefit: The dua of the servants of the Most Merciful. 'Qurrata aʿyun' — comfort to the eyes — is among the highest expressions of joy in Arabic.

use: For a new marriage
title: Blessing for a Marriage
arabic: بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ وَبَارَكَ عَلَيْكَ وَجَمَعَ بَيْنَكُمَا فِي خَيْرٍ
translit: Bārakallāhu laka wa bāraka ʿalayka wa jamaʿa baynakumā fī khayr

translations.en: May Allah bless you, may He send blessings upon you, and may He join you together in goodness.
translations.ur: اللہ تمہیں برکت دے، تم پر برکت نازل کرے، اور تم دونوں کو خیر کے ساتھ ملائے۔

context: The Sunnah words of congratulation for a newly married couple.
whenToRecite: For your own marriage, or any union you wish to see blessed.
benefit: The Sunnah congratulation for a newly married couple. Recite for your own marriage or any union you wish to see blessed.

use: When you have wronged and turn back
title: A Dua of Repentance
arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَآ أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَٰسِرِينَ
translit: Rabbanā ẓalamnā anfusanā wa in lam taghfir lanā wa tarḥamnā lanakūnanna minal-khāsirīn

translations.en: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers.
translations.ur: اے ہمارے رب! ہم نے اپنی جانوں پر ظلم کیا، اور اگر تو نے ہمیں نہ بخشا اور ہم پر رحم نہ کیا تو ہم ضرور نقصان اٹھانے والوں میں سے ہو جائیں گے۔

context: A prayer of return after a wrong. It names the fault plainly, 'we have wronged ourselves,' then rests entirely on Allah's forgiveness and mercy, recognizing that without them one is truly lost. It answers a slip by owning it, not by explaining it away.
story: These are the words of Adam ؑ and his wife after they ate from the one tree they had been forbidden in the Garden (Quran 2:35, 20:120). What the Quran preserves is not the lapse but the turning back: rather than shift the blame, they took ownership and asked their Lord for forgiveness and mercy in these very words (Quran 7:23). Allah accepted their repentance, as He tells us, 'Then Adam received words from his Lord, and He accepted his repentance.' (Quran 2:37)
whenToRecite: After any wrong or shortcoming, when you want to own it and turn back to Allah rather than excuse it.
benefit: The words of Adam ؑ after the slip in the Garden (Quran 7:23). He took immediate ownership and asked for forgiveness and mercy, and Allah accepted his repentance (Quran 2:37).

use: When you are overpowered and need help
title: Asking Allah for Help
arabic: أَنِّي مَغْلُوبٌ فَٱنتَصِرْ
translit: Annī maghlūbun fantaṣir

translations.en: Indeed, I am overpowered, so help me.
translations.ur: بے شک میں مغلوب ہوں، پس تو مدد فرما۔

context: A plea stripped to its essence: an admission of being overwhelmed, and a single word handing the outcome to Allah. There is no argument and no plan, only the turn to the One who can answer.
story: After calling his people for centuries against ridicule and threats, Nuh ؑ called upon his Lord with these words (Quran 54:9-10). Allah answered: 'So We opened the gates of heaven with rain pouring down.' (Quran 54:11)
whenToRecite: When you have done what you can and are outmatched by the situation, so the help has to come from Allah.
benefit: The cry of Nuh ؑ after centuries of rejection (Quran 54:10): an admission of being overpowered and a turn to Allah for help. Allah answered him.

use: For your parents and the believers
title: Forgiveness for Parents and Believers
arabic: رَبِّ ٱغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِمَن دَخَلَ بَيْتِيَ مُؤْمِنًا وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَٰتِ وَلَا تَزِدِ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا تَبَارًا
translit: Rabbi-ghfir lī wa li-wālidayya wa liman dakhala baytiya mu'minan wa lil-mu'minīna wal-mu'mināti wa lā tazidiẓ-ẓālimīna illā tabārā

translations.en: My Lord, forgive me and my parents and whoever enters my house as a believer, and the believing men and the believing women. And do not increase the wrongdoers except in ruin.
translations.ur: اے میرے رب! مجھے بخش دے، اور میرے ماں باپ کو، اور ہر اس شخص کو جو ایمان کے ساتھ میرے گھر میں داخل ہو، اور تمام مومن مردوں اور مومن عورتوں کو۔ اور ظالموں کو ہلاکت کے سوا کسی چیز میں زیادہ نہ کر۔

context: A dua that widens outward: from oneself, to one's parents, to everyone who enters the house in faith, to all believing men and women across time. The closing line against the wrongdoers came only at the end of a long life of calling them to Allah, not in place of it.
story: At the close of Surah Nuh, the Quran records this as the supplication Nuh ؑ made for his parents and the believers (Quran 71:28). Classical commentators read 'my house' as either his place of worship or the ark itself, so the prayer reaches everyone who trusted the call.
whenToRecite: When remembering your parents and the believers in your dua, those living and those who have passed.
benefit: The dua at the end of Surah Nuh (71:28): forgiveness for oneself, one's parents, and all believing men and women.

use: When others deny or dismiss you
title: Asking for Support When Denied
arabic: رَبِّ ٱنصُرْنِي بِمَا كَذَّبُونِ
translit: Rabbi-nṣurnī bimā kadhdhabūn

translations.en: My Lord, support me because they have denied me.
translations.ur: اے میرے رب! تو میری مدد فرما، کیونکہ انہوں نے مجھے جھٹلایا ہے۔

context: Not a prayer against the deniers, but a request to be carried through their denial. It hands the weight of being disbelieved back to Allah and asks only for His support.
story: Nuh ؑ made this dua while building the ark, mocked by his people for building a ship far from any sea (Quran 23:26-27). The verses that follow record Allah's instruction to build it 'under Our eyes,' and the rescue that came after.
whenToRecite: When you are doubted or dismissed for doing what you know to be right.
benefit: The dua of Nuh ؑ while building the ark amid mockery (Quran 23:26): a request to be supported through others' denial.

use: When you set out on a journey
title: A Dua for Travelling
arabic: رَبِّ أَنزِلْنِي مُنزَلًا مُبَارَكًا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلْمُنزِلِينَ
translit: Rabbi anzilnī munzalan mubārakan wa anta khayrul-munzilīn

translations.en: My Lord, let me come to land at a blessed place, for You are the best to bring [me] to rest.
translations.ur: اے میرے رب! مجھے بابرکت جگہ پر اتار، اور تو سب سے بہتر اتارنے والا ہے۔

context: A dua for arrival: it asks not only to reach the destination but to be brought down into a place of blessing, naming Allah as the best of those who give a resting place.
story: The Quran gives these words to Nuh ؑ in connection with the ark (Quran 23:29). The commentators differ on whether he said them on boarding or on coming to land, so it is recited at the start of a journey, at its end, or both.
whenToRecite: When setting out on a journey by any means, or on reaching your destination.
benefit: The dua of Nuh ؑ tied to the ark (Quran 23:29): to be brought to land in a place of blessing. Recited when travelling, on departure or arrival.

use: When you have overstepped and seek mercy
title: Seeking Refuge and Mercy
arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أَسْأَلَكَ مَا لَيْسَ لِي بِهِ عِلْمٌ وَإِلَّا تَغْفِرْ لِي وَتَرْحَمْنِي أَكُن مِنَ ٱلْخَٰسِرِينَ
translit: Rabbi innī aʿūdhu bika an asʾalaka mā laysa lī bihī ʿilmun wa illā taghfir lī wa tarḥamnī akun minal-khāsirīn

translations.en: My Lord, I seek refuge in You from asking You for that of which I have no knowledge. And unless You forgive me and have mercy upon me, I will be among the losers.
translations.ur: اے میرے رب! میں تیری پناہ مانگتا ہوں کہ تجھ سے وہ مانگوں جس کا مجھے علم نہیں۔ اور اگر تو نے مجھے نہ بخشا اور مجھ پر رحم نہ کیا تو میں نقصان اٹھانے والوں میں سے ہو جاؤں گا۔

context: A dua of restraint before Allah: it withdraws a question that should not have been asked and seeks refuge in Him from overstepping, then rests on His forgiveness and mercy. Its closing words echo the dua of Adam ؑ.
story: When the flood came, Nuh ؑ asked Allah about his drowned son, and was answered, 'He is not of your family; his work was other than righteous, so ask Me not for that of which you have no knowledge.' (Quran 11:45-46) Nuh ؑ replied with this prayer (Quran 11:47), turning the correction into repentance.
whenToRecite: When you realize you have asked for or assumed something beyond what was yours to, and you turn back to Allah.
benefit: The dua of Nuh ؑ after questioning Allah about his son (Quran 11:47): seeking refuge from overstepping, and resting on Allah's forgiveness and mercy.
